Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results state 'The Arabidopsis protein At-FAX1 (At3g57280...)'.

Applied_Species.reason: The functional mutant analyses were performed in Arabidopsis; the paper repeatedly states analyses in Arabidopsis and Materials specify Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Abstract/Materials identify the study species as Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Materials specify Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia (Col-0), and Results use Col-0 as wild type.

Source_Species.reason: The core gene characterized is At-FAX1 from Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: At-FAX1 is from Arabidopsis thaliana as stated throughout the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results describe FAX1 as grouping to the Tmemb_14 superfamily, mention InterPro/UPF0136, and note predicted hydrophobic/helical membrane-spanning domains; chloroplast inner envelope localization is shown in Fig. 2.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Fig. 2 and Results explicitly conclude FAX1 inserts into the inner envelope membrane of plastids/chloroplasts.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support that FAX1 mediates plastid fatty acid export and that knockout reduces ER-derived lipids, TAGs, C29 ketone waxes, biomass, and male fertility, while overexpression increases TAGs and biomass.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measure and discuss triacylglycerol oils, C29 ketone wax components, and pollen wall/coat materials derived from fatty acids/lipids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are explicitly fatty acid-derived lipids/waxes/pollen wall components.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: TAGs were measured in leaves and flowers; C29 ketone wax in stem epidermis/cuticle; pollen wall defects in anther/pollen outer wall are shown by microscopy.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: TAGs are discussed as storage oils, cuticular waxes as part of the cuticular barrier, and pollen wall/coat formation as required for male fertility; these functions are stated or directly inferable from the Results/Discussion.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present: T-DNA knockouts, complementation, 35S overexpression, GFP targeting, immunoblotting, lipidomics/MS, wax analysis, microscopy/TEM, and yeast complementation assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states in Discussion/S5 Fig. that At-FAX1 transcripts are present in all developmental stages and peak in leaf tissues and early pollen development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: S5 Fig./Discussion support high expression in cauline leaves, sepals, and early pollen development in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The 35S promoter was used for complementation and overexpression constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Standard Arabidopsis growth conditions are specified in Methods, and yeast assays used α-linolenic acid or cerulenin plus exogenous fatty acids.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper mentions co-expression of At-FAX1 with LACS4 and possible cooperation with ACS/LACS proteins in Discussion, but no direct physical interaction was tested.

Omics_Data.reason: DNA microarray data accession E-MTAB-3090 is explicitly given, and lipidomics used UPLC-Orbitrap MS and UPLC-qTOF MS.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Knockout phenotypes of severe male sterility, reduced biomass, and altered lipid homeostasis are directly shown in Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper proposes FAX1 as a promising tool to increase oil/biofuel production and suggests stacking/coupling with other pathways in Discussion, but the specific phrasing 'seed-specific deployment to minimize developmental penalties' is a forward-looking extrapolation rather than directly tested.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are faithful to the Results: chloroplast inner envelope Tmemb_14 protein, control of plastid FA export/lipid homeostasis by loss/gain of function, and requirement for pollen wall, stem ketone wax, biomass, and TAG accumulation.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects the paper: FAX1 is the experimentally centered gene in Results; pea Ps-FAX1 was cloned and used comparatively/localization-related, while metabolic phenotypes were tested in Arabidopsis.
